doc.members:
Returns the local config directory for the current opened project.
Returns the global config directory for the current user. This directory is shared between all the projects and should be used for settings that are not project specific.
Load the settings from file
Save current settings to file
Current value between 0 - 1 of the joystick dead zone
Current value between 0 - 1 of the triggers dead zone
Sets the current value between 0 - 1 of the joystick dead zone
Sets the current value between 0 - 1 of the triggers dead zone
Returns the name of the controller or null if controllerNumber does no exist
Number of active controllers
Returns the name of the keyboard key
Returns the name of the controller button
Returns the name of the mouse button
Returns the number of the groups
Returns true if it removes group called groupName
<member name="M:CometEngine::InputSettings::InputSettings::GetGroups" return="array">
Returns an array of all InputGroup
Destroy the immutable scene
Number of scenes currently loaded
Executes a shell command on the operating system and returns the exit code.
Executes a shell command and returns the standard output result along with the exit status.
Number of scenes added in build settings
The scene active
Returns a new scene that is opened additively.
Returns the scene with index in the scenes loaded list
Returns the scene with that buildSettingsIndex
Removes every unused resource from memory
Searches through the Scenes loaded for a Scene with the given name.
Searches through the Scenes loaded for a Scene with the given path.
Loads the scene with x buildIndex
Loads the scene with name
Move every Entity in toMoveOut to toMoveIn and then removes toMoveOut scene
Set a new active scene
Unload the scene with index in the loaded scenes list. Only works if more than one scene is loaded
Unload the scene. Only works if more than one scene is loaded
Load a scene in background and return a handler to this operation
Load a scene in background and return a handler to this operation
<member name="M:CometEngine::SceneManagement::SceneManager::GetSceneAsyncOperations" return="array">
Gets all the current active scene async operations.
Returns the index of the scene
Returns true if the scene is loaded
Returns the name of the scene
Returns the local path of the scene --> Assets/...
Returns the number of roots of the scene
Returns true if the scene root is enabled
Set the root state, if disabled, all entitys in this scene are disabled
<member name="M:CometEngine::SceneManagement::Scene::GetRoots(Scene)" return="array">
Returns an array of Entities that contains every root of the scene
<member name="M:CometEngine::SceneManagement::Scene::GetRoots(Scene)" return="array">
Returns an array of Entities that contains every root of the scene
Moves the given entity to the given scene
Enumeration of all available horizontal alignments.
Lines start at the left edge of the rect.
Lines are centered at the middle of the rect.
Lines end at the right edge of the rect.
Enumeration of all flags available for the navigation.
No navigation.
Navigation auto horizontal.
Navigation auto vertical.
Navigation to every side to a fixed Entity.
Automatic navigation.
All navigation types valid.
Enumeration of all available overflow text modes.
Text can overflow the rect.
Text can not overflow the rect.
Enumeration of the differents UI rendering modes.
UI is rendered on the screen.
UI is rendered in the world as it were a normal object.
Enumeration of the differents UI scale modes.
UI elements retain the same pixel size regardless of screen size
UI elements are bigger the bigger screen is
Enumeration of all available match modes.
Scales the canvas area with the width as reference, the height as reference, or something in between.
Expands the canvas area either horizontally or vertically, so the size of the canvas will never be smaller than the reference.
Crops the canvas area either horizontally or vertically, so the size of the canvas will never be larger than the reference.
Enumeration of all available screen spaces.
UI is rendered on the real screen
UI is rendered only on the given camera screen
Enumeration of all available selectable transitions.
No transitions will be performed.
Image will change its color depending on the state.
Image will change its sprite depending on the state.
Animator will change its state depending on the transition state.
Enumeration of all available vertical alignments.
Text starts at the top edge of the rect.
Text is centered at the middle of the rect.
Text ends at the bottom edge of the rect.
Interface to inherit from a custom class that wants to be used as parameters for a coroutine.
Base interface for every yield instruction.
Interface to inherit from when a custom class wants to be serialized and unserialized using JsonObject.
Interface to inherit from a CometBehaviour in order to listen to the start of a drag action on the UI Selectable of the Entity.
Callback called at the start of a drag action on the UI Element of the Entity.
Interface to inherit from a CometBehaviour in order to listen the deselect action on the UI Selectable of the Entity.
Callback called when the UI Element of the Entity is deselected.
Interface to inherit from a CometBehaviour in order to listen to a drag action on the UI Selectable of the Entity.
Callback called when a drag action is performed on the UI Element of the Entity.
Interface to inherit from a CometBehaviour in order to listen to the end of a drag action on the UI Selectable of the Entity.
Callback called at the end of a drag action on the UI Element of the Entity.
Interface to inherit from a CometBehaviour in order to listen Pointer click on the UI Selectable of the Entity.
Callback called when the pointer is clicked to the UI Element of the Entity.
Interface to inherit from a CometBehaviour in order to listen Pointer down on the UI Selectable of the Entity.
Callback called when the pointer is down to the UI Element of the Entity.
Interface to inherit from a CometBehaviour in order to listen Pointer Enter on the UI Selectable of the Entity.
Callback called when the pointer enters to the UI Element of the Entity.
Interface to inherit from a CometBehaviour in order to listen Pointer Exit on the UI Selectable of the Entity.
Callback called when the pointer exits to the UI Element of the Entity.
Interface to inherit from a CometBehaviour in order to listen Pointer up on the UI Selectable of the Entity.
Callback called when the pointer is up to the UI Element of the Entity.
Interface to inherit from a CometBehaviour in order to listen the select action on the UI Selectable of the Entity.
Callback called when the UI Element of the Entity is selected.
Interface to inherit from a CometBehaviour in order to listen Submit action on the UI Selectable of the Entity.
Callback called when the UI Element is submitted.
The aspect ratios availables for a video player.
No scaling is used. The video is centered on the destination rectangle.
Scale the source to fit the destination rectangle vertically. The source aspect ratio is preserved.
Scale the source to fit the destination rectangle horizontally. The source aspect ratio is preserved.
Scales down the in order so all the video is seen inside the rectangle. The source aspect ratio is preserved.
Scales up in order to cover all the destination rectangle. The source aspect ratio is preserved.
Scale both horizontally or vertically to fit the destination rectangle. The source aspect ratio isn’t preserved.
All the available audio outputs modes for the video player.
No audio.
The audio is outputted directly.
The audio goes through an audio source.
Available values of how the video should be rendered.
Video is rendered at the far plane of a camera.
Video is rendered at the near plane of a camera.
Video is rendered at a render texture.
The source type of the video.
The video to play is a video resource clip.
The video to play is an url or a path.
Enumeration of all available subtitle text data types.
Undefined format.
The text data is in ASS (Advanced SubStation Alpha).
The text data is simple text.
Struct that holds the subtitle data send by the videoplayer.
The subtitle text.
The format of the data.
The duration of this text in ms.
The box index where this texts belongs to.
Enumeration of the different color mode calculations.
Blend between the two marks.
Fixed value of the previous mark.
Xbox Share | PS5 Microphone | Switch Pro Capture.
"Xbox Elite Padle 1.
Xbox Elite Padle 2.
Xbox Elite Padle 3.
Xbox Elite Padle 4.
PS4/PS5 Touchpad.
Additional Button 1.
Additional Button 2.
Additional Button 3.
Additional Button 4.
Additional Button 5.
Enumeration that contains all available modes for a texture.
Texture will be used as a spritesheet.
Texture will be used as a normal map.
Total count of texture type modes.
Called when application is closed
Gets the priority of the background loading data
Sets the priority of the background loading data
Company Name
Get the user-and-app-specific path where files can be written, on windows should be something like this: "C:\\Users\\userName\\AppData\\Roaming\\My Company\\My Program Name\\"
Returns the global path of the exe folder
Returns the folder for data assets
True if the game is running in the comet engine editor
Product Name
Gets if the application stop when the window has no focus and false if it does not
Sets if the application stop when the window has no focus and false if it does not
Gets the desired frame rate, set it to 0 to disable caping fps
Sets the desired frame rate, set it to 0 to disable caping fps
The current frame rate
Engine Version
The product version
When called, app will quit
Opens the url in the default browser
Gets the current vsync state
Sets a new vsync state
Save current settings to file
Load the settings from file
Gets the name of the current output device
Sets the current output device
Gets the master Volume that ranges from 0.0F to 1.0F
Sets the master Volume that ranges from 0.0F to 1.0F
Gets the rolloff scale which influences the amount of attenuation that is applied to 3D sounds. The rolloff factor can range from 0.0 to 10.0, where 0 is no rolloff. 1.0 is the default rolloff factor set, the value which we also experience in the real world. A value of 2 would mean twice the real-world rolloff.
Sets the rolloff scale which influences the amount of attenuation that is applied to 3D sounds. The rolloff factor can range from 0.0 to 10.0, where 0 is no rolloff. 1.0 is the default rolloff factor set, the value which we also experience in the real world. A value of 2 would mean twice the real-world rolloff.
Gets the value between 0 and 5 which multiplies the doppler effect. Default value is 1.0, which is the real world doppler effect, and 5.0f would be five times the real world doppler effect
Sets a value between 0 and 5 which multiplies the doppler effect. Default value is 1.0, which is the real world doppler effect, and 5.0f would be five times the real world doppler effect
Gets the default distance at which a 3D Sound is at its maximum volume
Sets the default distance at which a 3D Sound is at its maximum volume
Gets the default distance at which a 3D Sound is inaudible
Sets the default distance at which a 3D Sound is inaudible
Resume all sounds
Pause all sounds
Stop all sounds
Save current audio settings to file
Load the audio file settings
<member name="M:CometEngine::AudioSystem::GetOutputDevices()" return="array">
Array of the output devices, it can be null
Gets if the cursor is being rendered and false if not
Sets if the cursor is being rendered and false if not
Set a system default cursor
Set the hotspot and texture for the cursor, setting texture as null will set the cursor as the default in Player Settings
Gets if this build is in development mode.
Gets if objects will be created with light materials if true
Sets if objects will be created with light materials if true
Gets if you want to load all textures async
Sets if you want to load all textures async
Gets the anti-aliasing level
Sets the anti-aliasing level
Save current graphics settings to file
Load the graphics file settings
Called when a controller device is connected
Called when a controller device is disconnected
Returns true if any keyboard key is currently being held down.
True if any key is pressed.
Returns true if any keyboard key was pressed down this frame.
True if any key went down.
Returns true if any keyboard key was released this frame.
True if any key went up.
Returns true if any mouse button is currently being held down.
True if any mouse button is pressed.
Returns true if any mouse button is down
Returns true if any mouse button is up
Returns the current mouse position in screen coordinates. Top-left is (0,0).
The mouse position as a Vector2.
Returns the mouse motion
Returns the mouse scroll motion
Returns true if the specified key is currently being held down.
The key to check. True if the key is pressed.
Returns true during the frame the user starts pressing down the specified key.
The key to check. True if the key went down this frame.
Returns true during the frame the user releases the specified key.
The key to check. True if the key was released this frame.
Returns true if the specified mouse button is currently being held down.
The mouse button to check. True if the button is pressed.
Returns true the first frame the mouse button is pressed
Returns true when the mouse button is released
Returns the type of the specified controller.
The controller index (starting from 1). The ControllerType enum value.
<member name="M:CometEngine::Input::GetControllersConnected()" return="array">
Returns an array of identifiers for all currently connected controllers.
An array of ControllerNumber values.
Returns true if the specified button is currently held down on any connected controller.
The button to check. True if the button is pressed on any controller.
Returns true if the specified button is currently held down on a specific controller.
The button to check. The controller index (starting from 1). True if the button is pressed on the specified controller.
Returns true during the frame the specified button is pressed down on any connected controller.
The button to check. True if the button went down this frame on any controller.
Returns true when the controller button is down only in the controller that has controllerNumber. The controller number one has number 1, controller number two has number 2... and so on
Returns true when the controller button is up in any controller
Returns true during the frame the specified button is released on a specific controller.
The button to check. The controller index (starting from 1). True if the button was released this frame on the specified controller.
Triggers vibration on all connected controllers.
Vibration intensity (0 to 1). Duration of the vibration in milliseconds.
Triggers vibration on a specific connected controller.
Vibration intensity (0 to 1). Duration of the vibration in milliseconds. The controller index (starting from 1).
Returns the trigger value between 0 to 1. The controller number one has number 1, controller number two has number 2... and so on
Returns the axis value between 0 to 1. The controller number one has number 1, controller number two has number 2... and so on
Find the first action called actionName and returns true if it is done
Returns the number of currently active touches
Returns the touch data for a specific touch ID
Returns the touch data at a specific index
Returns true if a pinch gesture is currently active
Returns the pinch zoom scale
Called when a touch starts
Called when a touch moves
Called when a touch ends
Called when a pinch gesture starts
Called when a pinch gesture moves
Called when a pinch gesture ends
Represents a touch point with its data
The unique identifier of the touch
The position of the touch in screen space
The motion (delta) of the touch from the last frame
The pressure of the touch, normalized between 0 and 1
The current state of the touch (down, up, moving, etc)
The number of iterations of the physics solver when considering objects' velocities
The number of iterations of the physics solver when considering objects' velocities
The number of iterations of the physics solver when considering objects' positions
The number of iterations of the physics solver when considering objects' positions
Acceleration due to gravity
Acceleration due to gravity
Should raycast hit triggers?
Should raycast hit triggers?
The maximum angular speed of a rigid-body per physics update
The maximum angular speed of a rigid-body per physics update
Any collisions with a relative linear velocity below this threshold will be treated as inelastic
Any collisions with a relative linear velocity below this threshold will be treated as inelastic
The maximum linear position correction used when solving constraints
The maximum linear position correction used when solving constraints
The maximum angular position correction used when solving constraints
The maximum angular position correction used when solving constraints
The maximum linear speed of a rigid-body per physics update
The maximum linear speed of a rigid-body per physics update
The scale factor that controls how fast overlaps are resolved
The scale factor that controls how fast overlaps are resolved
The scale factor that controls how fast TOI overlaps are resolved
The scale factor that controls how fast TOI overlaps are resolved
The color used by the gizmos to show all awake colliders
The color used by the gizmos to show all awake colliders
The color used by the gizmos to show all asleep colliders
The color used by the gizmos to show all asleep colliders
Sets the color used by the gizmos to show all Collider axis-aligned bounding boxes
Sets the color used by the gizmos to show all Collider axis-aligned bounding boxes
Should the collider gizmos show the AABBs for each collider?
Should the collider gizmos show the AABBs for each collider?
Should the collider gizmos show the sleep-state for each collider?
Should the collider gizmos show the sleep-state for each collider?
Should the collider gizmos always be shown even when they are not selected?
Should the collider gizmos always be shown even when they are not selected?
A rigid-body cannot sleep if its angular velocity is above this tolerance
A rigid-body cannot sleep if its angular velocity is above this tolerance
A rigid-body cannot sleep if its linear velocity is above this tolerance
A rigid-body cannot sleep if its linear velocity is above this tolerance
The time in seconds that a rigid-body must be still before it will go to sleep
The time in seconds that a rigid-body must be still before it will go to sleep
The default contact offset of the newly created colliders
The default contact offset of the newly created colliders
Gets the current seed for the random numbers
Sets the seed for the random numbers
Returns random int between min [inclusive] and max [inclusive]
Returns random float between min [inclusive] and max [inclusive]
Returns random double between min [inclusive] and max [inclusive]
Load a scene from a RuntimeAssets folder from the project, this method dos not change the scene. Use CometEngine.SceneManagement to change the scene
Unload all assets previously loaded, this will unload the asset once any object is using it
<member name="M:CometEngine::RuntimeAssets::LoadAllScenes(string)" return="array">
Loads all the scenes at the given path. The path is relative to the RuntimeAssets folder and file extension should not be included. This method does not change the scene. Use CometEngine.SceneManagement to change the scene. If the path points to a file, it will return an array with a single scene. If the path points to a folder, it will return an array with all the scenes in that folder and its subfolders.
<member name="M:CometEngine::RuntimeAssets::GetFilesAtDirectory(string)" return="array">
Gets all files at a directory from the RuntimeAssets folder. The path is relative to the RuntimeAssets folder.
<member name="M:CometEngine::RuntimeAssets::GetDirectoriesAtDirectory(string)" return="array">
Gets all directories at a directory from the RuntimeAssets folder. The path is relative to the RuntimeAssets folder. Only returns directories at the specified path, not in subfolders.
Saves a screen capture of the current game scene
The last frame seconds
The timeScale independent last frame seconds
The total numbers of frames since the start of the game
The total time since the start of the game
The total dependent scale time at the start of the frame
The total independent scale time at the start of the frame
The total dependent scale time since the start of the game
The total independent scale time since the start of the game
The scale at which time passes
The scale at which time passes
The time passed since last scene loaded
Time in seconds at which physics updates
Time in seconds at which physics updates
The total time dependent scale time in seconds passed since last FixedUpdate
The total time independent scale time in seconds passed since last FixedUpdate
Framerate-independent interval that indicates when particles are updated.
Framerate-independent interval that indicates when particles are updated.
Class that manages the UniqueInstances
Destroys the unique instance objects.
Create the unique insatance objects. If they are already created, this method will do nothing.
Removes the current unique instance objects and then creates it again.
Called when window loses the focus (in editor window means the game panel)
Called when window gains the focus (in editor window means the game panel)
Called when window is resized (in editor window means the engine window)
Called when window is maximized (only called on build)
Called when window is minimized (only called on build)
Called when window is restored (only called on build)
The width of the window in pixels
The width of the window in pixels
The height of the window in pixels
The height of the window in pixels
The initial position mode of the window
The initial position mode of the window
The initial position of the window on the screen in pixels
The initial position of the window on the screen in pixels
The initial screen index where the window will be created
The initial screen index where the window will be created
True if the window is always on top of other windows
True if the window is always on top of other windows
The position of the window on the screen in pixels
The position of the window on the screen in pixels
The minimum size of the window in pixels
The minimum size of the window in pixels
The maximum size of the window in pixels
The maximum size of the window in pixels
True if the window is bordered
True if the window is bordered
True if the window can be resized
True if the window can be resized
True if the window is focused
True if the window is minimized
True if the window is minimized
True if the window is maximized
Mode of the window
Mode of the window
Gets the window resolution
Loads the window settings
Saves the current window settings
Sets the window resolution
Struct that represents a Color with the four behaviours (red, green, blue and alpha) that range between 0.0F to 1.0F.
Creates a color with specific RGB values.
Red component (0 to 1). Green component (0 to 1). Blue component (0 to 1).
Creates a color with specific RGBA values.
Red component (0 to 1). Green component (0 to 1). Blue component (0 to 1). Alpha component (0 to 1).
Creates a color by copying another color and applying a different alpha value.
The source color to copy RGB from. The new alpha value (0 to 1).
Red component.
Green component.
Blue component.
Alpha component.
Returns a grayscale version of the current color.
The grayscale color.
Converts the color components to a readable string format.
A string representing the color.
Returns the value of the largest component (R, G, or B).
The maximum component value.
RGBA (0,0,0,1)
RGBA (0,0,1,1)
RGBA (0,0,0,0)
RGBA (0,1,1,1)
RGBA (0.5,0.5,0.5,1)
RGBA (0.5,0.5,0.5,1)
RGBA (0,1,0,1)
RGBA (1,0,1,1)
RGBA (1,0,0,1)
RGBA (1,1,1,1)
RGBA (1, 0.92, 0.016, 1)
Represents an alpha mark for a ColorGradient object.
Position to the gradient, value between 0.0F to 1.0F.
Alpha behaviour.
Represents a color mark for a ColorGradient object.
Position to the gradient, value between 0.0F to 1.0F.
Red behaviour.
Green behaviour.
Blue behaviour.
<member name="M:CometEngine::LayerMask::GetMultiMask(array)" return="int64">
Return the mask
Return the mask
Return the mask of the number of a layer
Return the number of the layer name
Return the name of the layerNumber
Return the name of the mask
Value of the mask
Constant that holds all the possible layers.
(0, 0, 0, 1)
Returns the dot product of two quaternions.
The other quaternion. The dot product value.
Quaternion eulerAngles in degrees
Returns a quaternion with magnitud of 1
Normalizes this quaternion in-place so its magnitude becomes 1.
The original magnitude before normalization.
Creates a rotation which rotates angle degrees around axis.
The angle in degrees. The axis of rotation. The resulting quaternion.
Creates a rotation which rotates from fromDirection to toDirection.
The source direction. The target direction. The resulting quaternion.
Set the x, y, z and w of this quaternion
Gets the axis and angle of this quaternion rotation.
Output vector for the rotation axis. Output float for the rotation angle in degrees.
Returns the angle of this quaternion
Creates a rotation which rotates z degrees around the Z axis, x degrees around the X axis, and y degrees around the Y axis (in that order).
The X rotation in degrees. The Y rotation in degrees. The Z rotation in degrees. The resulting quaternion.
Inverse this quaternion
Linearly interpolates between two rotations.
The starting rotation. The ending rotation. The interpolation factor (0 to 1). The interpolated quaternion.
Linearly interpolates between two rotations without clamping the interpolation factor.
The starting rotation. The ending rotation. The interpolation factor. The interpolated quaternion.
Spherically interpolates between two rotations.
The starting rotation. The ending rotation. The interpolation factor (0 to 1). The interpolated quaternion.
Spherically interpolates between two rotations without clamping the interpolation factor.
The starting rotation. The ending rotation. The interpolation factor. The interpolated quaternion.
Return the inverted quaternion
Return the angle between two quaternions in radians
(0, -1)
Returns the length (magnitude) of the vector.
The length of the vector.
Returns the squared length of the vector.
The squared length.
Returns a copy of this vector with a magnitude of 1.
The normalized vector.
Sets the X and Y components of this vector.
The new X component. The new Y component.
Returns the squared distance between two vectors.
The destination vector. The squared distance.
Returns the Euclidean distance between two vectors.
The destination vector. The distance value.
Returns the dot product of two vectors.
The other vector. The dot product value.
Linearly interpolates between two vectors.
The destination vector. The interpolation factor (0 to 1). The interpolated Vector2.
Returns a vector that is made from the largest components of two vectors.
The other vector to compare against. A vector with the maximum components.
Returns a vector that is made from the smallest components of two vectors.
The other vector to compare against. A vector with the minimum components.
Linearly interpolates between two vectors using another vector as interpolation factors for each axis.
The destination vector. The interpolated Vector2.
Returns the unsigned angle in degrees between two vectors.
The destination vector. The angle in degrees.
Returns the signed angle in degrees between two vectors.
The destination vector. The signed angle in degrees (-180 to 180).
Normalizes this vector in-place so its length becomes 1.
The original length of the vector before normalization.
(-1, 0)
(1, 1)
(1, 0)
(0, 1)
(0, 0)
Returns a new Vector2 with the absolute value of each component.
The absolute value vector.
(0, -1)
Returns the length of the vector
Returns the squared length of the vector
Set this vector with new x,y and z
Returns the squared distance
Returns the distance
Returns a vector that is made from the largest behaviours of two vectors.
Returns a vector that is made from the smallest behaviours of two vectors.
Returns a new Vector2i perpendicular to vector
(-1, 0)
(1, 1)
(1, 0)
(0, 1)
(0, 0)
Returns a new Vector2i with absolut value of each behaviour
Linearly interpolates between two vectors.
The destination vector. The interpolation factor (0 to 1). The interpolated Vector3.
Linearly interpolates between two vectors
Projects a vector onto another vector.
Spherically interpolates between two vectors
Spherically interpolates between two vectors without clamping the interpolation factor.
The destination vector. The interpolation factor. The interpolated Vector3.
Returns a vector that is made from the largest components of two vectors.
The other vector to compare against. A vector with the maximum components.
Returns a vector that is made from the smallest components of two vectors.
The other vector to compare against. A vector with the minimum components.
(0, 0, -1)
(0, -1, 0)
(0, 0, 1)
(-1, 0, 0)
Returns a new Vector3 with the absolute value of each component.
The absolute value vector.
(1, 1, 1)
(1, 0, 0)
(0, 1, 0)
(0, 0, 0)
Returns the length (magnitude) of the vector.
The length of the vector.
Returns the squared length of the vector.
The squared length.
Returns a copy of this vector with a magnitude of 1.
The normalized vector.
Normalizes this vector in-place so its length becomes 1.
The original length of the vector before normalization.
Sets the components of this vector.
The new X component. The new Y component. The new Z component.
Returns the dot product of two vectors.
The other vector. The dot product value.
Returns the cross product of two vectors.
The other vector. The cross product value.
Returns the squared distance between two vectors.
The destination vector. The squared distance.
Returns the Euclidean distance between two vectors.
The destination vector. The distance value.
Returns the unsigned angle in degrees between two vectors.
The destination vector. The angle in degrees.
Returns the signed angle in degrees between two vectors, rotating around the specified axis.
The destination vector. The axis to rotate around. The signed angle in degrees (-180 to 180).
Returns a vector that is made from the largest behaviours of two vectors.
Returns a vector that is made from the smallest behaviours of two vectors.
(0, 0, -1)
(0, -1, 0)
(0, 0, 1)
(-1, 0, 0)

(1, 1, 1)

(0, 0, 0)
Returns the length of the vector
Returns the squared length of the vector

Enumeration of all viewport aspect ratios.
Free aspect ratio.
16:9 aspect ratio.
4:3 aspect ratio.
21:9 aspect ratio.
16:10 aspect ratio.
3:2 aspect ratio.
5:4 aspect ratio.
Custom aspect ratio.
Returns the viewport aspect ratio.
Sets the viewport aspect ratio.
Returns the custom aspect ratio.
Sets the custom aspect ratio.
Returns true if aspect ratio is maintained on fullscreen.
Sets if aspect ratio should be maintained on fullscreen.
Returns true if aspect ratio is maintained on fullscreen desktop.
Sets if aspect ratio should be maintained on fullscreen desktop.
Checks if the given path exists.
Creates the given directory or directories if needed.
Removes the given file/folder.
Removes all files/folder in the given path recursive.
Checks if a path is a directory.
Loads the given file into a string.
Saves the given string into a file.
<member name="M:CometEngine::FileSystem::GetDirectoriesAt(string)" return="array">
Returns an array of strings containing the names of the directories at the given path.
<member name="M:CometEngine::FileSystem::GetFilesAt(string)" return="array">
Returns an array of strings containing the names of the files at the given path.
Funcdef for a callback delegate with no parameters
Handler for managing delegates with no parameters. Allows adding, removing, and invoking multiple callbacks.
Creates a new CometDelegateHandler
Adds a callback to the handler
Removes a callback from the handler
Checks if a callback is registered in the handler
Returns the number of registered callbacks
Clears all registered callbacks
Invokes all registered callbacks
Funcdef for a callback delegate that receives a ControllerNumber parameter
Handler for managing delegates with ControllerNumber parameter. Allows adding, removing, and invoking multiple callbacks.
Creates a new CometDelegateHandlerControllerNumber
Adds a callback to the handler
Removes a callback from the handler
Checks if a callback is registered in the handler
Returns the number of registered callbacks
Clears all registered callbacks
Funcdef for a callback delegate that receives a float parameter
Handler for managing delegates with float parameter. Allows adding, removing, and invoking multiple callbacks.
Creates a new CometDelegateHandlerfloat
Adds a callback to the handler
Removes a callback from the handler
Checks if a callback is registered in the handler
Returns the number of registered callbacks
Clears all registered callbacks
Funcdef for a callback delegate that receives a uint64 parameter
Handler for managing delegates with uint64 parameter. Allows adding, removing, and invoking multiple callbacks.
Creates a new CometDelegateHandleruint64
Adds a callback to the handler
Removes a callback from the handler
Checks if a callback is registered in the handler
Returns the number of registered callbacks
Clears all registered callbacks
Generic event handler that accepts a typed parameter. Allows managing and invoking callbacks with a specific parameter type.
Creates a new CometEventArg generic event handler
Removes all persistent listeners registered to this event
Invokes all registered listeners with the provided parameter
Adds a runtime listener to the event
Removes a runtime listener from the event
Event handler that accepts no parameters. Allows managing and invoking callbacks without arguments.
Creates a new CometEvent
Removes all persistent listeners registered to this event
Removes all runtime listeners from this event
Removes all listeners (both persistent and runtime) from this event
Invokes all registered listeners
Adds a runtime listener to the event
Removes a runtime listener from the event
Begin a table. You MUST call EndTable() when closing this table to prevent issues.
Unique identifier for the table. Number of columns in the table. Configuration flags for table behavior. Total size of the table area. Width of the inner content area. True if the table is open and content should be submitted.
Ends a previously started table.
Moves to the next row in the table.
Flags for the row configuration. Minimum height for the row.
Moves to the next column in the table.
Returns true if the column is visible and content should be submitted.
Sets the current column index.
The index of the column to set as active. Returns true if the column index was set successfully.
Sets up a column configuration for the current table.
The column header label. Flags for column behavior. Initial width or weight of the column. Optional user-defined ID for identifying the column.
Submits the headers row.
Gets the number of columns in the current table.
The total number of columns.
Gets the name of a specific column.
The index of the column. The name of the column at the specified index.
Gets the current column index.
The index of the current column.
Gets the current row index.
The index of the current row.
Sets the background color for a table target.
The target area (e.g., row, cell). The color to apply. Optional column index if targeting a specific cell.
Sets up scroll freeze functionality for a table.
Number of columns to freeze from the left. Number of rows to freeze from the top.
Draws a table header with a label.
The text to display in the header.
Draws angled headers for the table.
Enables or disables a column in the table.
The index of the column. True to enable, false to disable.
Returns the index of the hovered column in the table.
The index of the column currently hovered by the mouse.
Push string into the ID stack. You MUST call PopID() when done.
The string identifier to push.
Push integer into the ID stack. You MUST call PopID() when done.
The integer identifier to push.
Pop from the ID stack.
Begin a combo box. You MUST call EndCombo() after a successful BeginCombo (when it returns true).
The label for the combo box. The currently selected value to display. Configuration flags. True if the combo is open and content should be submitted.
End a previously opened combo box.
A high-level combo box.
The label for the combo. The index of the currently selected item. String containing items separated by '\0'. Output boolean set to true if selection changed. Maximum number of items to show in the popup. The index of the selected item.
Begin a tree node. You MUST call TreePop() if this function returns true!
The label for the node. Tree node behavior flags. True if the node is open and children should be submitted.
Ends a previously started tree node.
Begin a tab bar. You MUST call EndTabBar() if it returns true!
Unique identifier for the tab bar. True if the tab bar is open and tab items should be submitted.
End a tab bar.
Begin a tab item. You MUST call EndTabItem() if it returns true!
The label displayed on the tab. True if the tab is selected and content should be submitted.
End a tab item.
Starts a group of items. You MUST call EndGroup() after a BeginGroup().
End a group.
Begin a child window area within the current window. You MUST call EndChild() if it returns true!
Unique identifier for the child area. Width of the child area. Use 0 for auto-size. Height of the child area. Use 0 for auto-size. Flags for child behavior. Window flags to apply to the child. True if the child area is visible and content should be submitted.
End a child area.
Disables the inputs for subsequent interactable items. You MUST call EndDisabled() afterwards.
If true, items within this block will be disabled.
Ends input disabling block.
Changes a style color. You MUST call PopStyleColor() to restore original state!
The index of the color style to change (ImGuiCol_ enum). The new color to apply.
Restores previous style colors.
The number of colors to pop from the stack.
Changes a style variable (float). You MUST call PopStyleVar() to restore original state!
The index of the float style variable to change (ImGuiStyleVar_ enum). The new float value to apply.
Changes a style variable (Vector2). You MUST call PopStyleVar() to restore original state!
The index of the Vector2 style variable to change (ImGuiStyleVar_ enum). The new Vector2 value to apply.
Restores previous style variables.
The number of variables to pop from the stack.
Renders generic text at the current cursor position.
The string to render.
Renders colored text.
The color of the text. The string to render.
Renders disabled text visually.
Renders word-wrapped text (automatically wraps to window width).
The string to render.
Draw a clickable button.
The label displayed on the button. The size of the button (0,0 for auto-size). Behavior flags for the button. True if the button was clicked this frame.
Draw a selectable text item that behaves like a list item.
The label for the item. Whether the item is currently highlighted as selected. Selectable behavior flags. True if the item was clicked this frame.
Sets the width of the next added element.
The width in pixels. Use a negative value to align from right.
Vertically align upcoming text baseline to FramePadding.
Draw a toggle checkbox.
The label for the checkbox. The current boolean state. The new boolean state after potential user interaction.
Draw a toggle checkbox with an explicit edited state output.
The label for the checkbox. The current boolean state. Output boolean set to true if the value was changed this frame. The new boolean state after potential user interaction.
Draws a horizontal slider for a floating point value.
The label for the slider. The current value. The minimum allowable value. The maximum allowable value. Slider behavior flags. The new value after potential user interaction.
Draws a horizontal slider for a floating point value with an explicit edited state output.
The label for the slider. The current value. The minimum allowable value. The maximum allowable value. Output boolean set to true if the value was changed this frame. Slider behavior flags. The new value after potential user interaction.
Draws an int slider.
Draws a horizontal slider for an integer value with an explicit edited state output.
The label for the slider. The current value. The minimum allowable value. The maximum allowable value. Output boolean set to true if the value was changed this frame. Slider behavior flags. The new value after potential user interaction.
Draws a numeric input field for a floating point value.
The label for the input field. The current value. Input behavior flags. The new value after potential user interaction.
Draws a numeric input field for a floating point value with an explicit edited state output.
The label for the input field. The current value. Output boolean set to true if the value was changed this frame. Input behavior flags. The new value after potential user interaction.
Draws a numeric input field for an integer value.
The label for the input field. The current value. Input behavior flags. The new value after potential user interaction.
Draws a numeric input field for an integer value with an explicit edited state output.
The label for the input field. The current value. Output boolean set to true if the value was changed this frame. Input behavior flags. The new value after potential user interaction.
Draws a text input field.
The label for the input field. The current text value. Input behavior flags. The new text string after potential user interaction.
Draws a text input field with an explicit edited state output.
The label for the input field. The current text value. Output boolean set to true if the text was changed this frame. Input behavior flags. The new text string after potential user interaction.
Draws a color picker edit widget.
The label for the color picker. The current color value. Color edit behavior flags. The new color value after potential user interaction.
Draws a color picker edit widget with an explicit edited state output.
The label for the color picker. The current color value. Output boolean set to true if the color was changed this frame. Color edit behavior flags. The new color value after potential user interaction.
Call between widgets or groups to layout them horizontally instead of vertically.
Offset from the left edge of the window. Spacing between widgets.
Add vertical spacing.
Separator, generally horizontal.
Move content position toward the right by a specified width.
Indentation width in pixels. Use 0 for default.
Move content position back toward the left.
Gets the internal window cursor X position relative to the window.
The current X coordinate of the cursor.
Gets the internal window cursor Y position relative to the window.
The current Y coordinate of the cursor.
Sets the internal window cursor X position relative to the window.
The new X coordinate for the cursor.
Sets the internal window cursor Y position relative to the window.
The new Y coordinate for the cursor.
Draws a specific property inspector logic for the given property name.
The name of the behaviour field to show in the inspector.
Draws the default inspector UI for all the properties in the behaviour.
Draws a CometEngine sprite UI thumbnail.
The sprite to display. The size of the thumbnail. Whether to preserve the sprite's aspect ratio. Whether to flip the sprite horizontally. Whether to flip the sprite vertically.
Draws a CometEngine texture UI thumbnail.
The texture to display. The size of the thumbnail. Whether to preserve the texture's aspect ratio. Whether to flip the texture horizontally. Whether to flip the texture vertically.
Forces the state of UI modifications to be saved (creates an Undo step).
Set the open state of the next item (e.g. tree node, collapsing header).
The desired open state. The condition for applying the state (e.g. Once, Always).
Set keyboard focus to the next item automatically.
Focus offset (0 for next item, -1 for previous).
Sets a simple tooltip to be shown when hovering the last item.
The tooltip text.
Get the dimensions of the current window.
The size of the current window as a Vector2.
Get the position of the current window.
The screen position of the current window.
Space available for content from current cursor position to bottom-right of window.
The available space as a Vector2.
Get the size of the last placed item.
The size of the last item.
Get the min rect bounds (top-left) of the last placed item.
The minimum bounds of the last item.
Get the max rect bounds (bottom-right) of the last placed item.
The maximum bounds of the last item.
Returns whether the last item is hovered by the mouse.
Configuration flags for the hover check. True if the last item is hovered.
Returns whether the last item is active (e.g. being clicked or held).
True if the last item is active.
Returns whether the last item is currently focused.
True if the last item is focused.
Returns whether the last item was clicked by a specific mouse button.
The mouse button to check. True if the last item was clicked.
Returns whether the last item is visible within the window or clipping area.
True if the last item is visible.
Returns whether the last item was edited (e.g. value changed).
True if the last item was edited.
Returns whether the last item was activated (just became active).
True if the last item was just activated.
Returns whether the last item was deactivated (just stopped being active).
True if the last item was just deactivated.
Returns whether the last item was deactivated specifically after an edit action.
True if deactivated after an edit.
Returns whether the last item (like a tree node) was toggled to the open state.
True if toggled open.
Returns whether any item in the current window is currently hovered.
True if any item is hovered.
Returns whether any item is active.
Returns whether any item is focused.
Returns whether the specified mouse button is currently held down.
The mouse button to check. True if the button is down.
Returns whether the specified mouse button was clicked this frame.
The mouse button to check. Whether to count repeating click events (when held). True if clicked.
Returns whether the specified mouse button was double clicked.
Returns whether the specified mouse button was released this frame.
The mouse button to check. True if released.
Returns whether the mouse is hovering over a specific rectangular area.
Top-left X coordinate. Top-left Y coordinate. Bottom-right X coordinate. Bottom-right Y coordinate. Whether to clip the check to the current window bounds. True if hovering the rect.
Returns whether the mouse position is currently valid (e.g. inside the OS window).
True if the mouse position is valid.
Gets the current mouse cursor position in window coordinates.
The mouse position as a Vector2.
Returns whether the mouse is currently dragging with a specific button.
The mouse button being held. Threshold distance before dragging is considered active. True if dragging.
Gets the distance the mouse has been dragged since the button was pressed.
The mouse button being held. Threshold distance before dragging is considered active. The drag delta as a Vector2.
Resets the current drag delta for the specified mouse button.
The mouse button to reset delta for.
Gets the current system representation of the mouse cursor style.
The current MouseCursor enum value.
Forces the system mouse cursor to a specific style.
The MouseCursor style to apply.
Clones an existing entity and all its components.
The entity to clone. The newly created entity clone.
Creates a new empty entity as a child of the specified parent.
The parent entity. The newly created entity.
Finds an entity by its name in the current scene.
The name of the entity to find. The found entity, or a null reference if not found.
Finds the first active entity with the specified tag.
The tag to search for. The found entity, or a null reference if not found.
<member name="M:CometEngine::Entity::FindEntitiesWithTag(string)" return="array">
<member name="M:CometEngine::Camera::GetAllCameras()" return="array">
<member name="M:CometEngine::InputSettings::InputSettings::GetGroups()" return="array">
<member name="M:CometEngine::Raycast(Vector2,Vector2,float distance,int layerMask,float minDepth,float maxDepth)" return="array">
<member name="M:CometEngine::QueryAABB(Vector2,Vector2,int layerMask,float minDepth,float maxDepth)" return="array">
<member name="M:CometEngine::RuntimeAssets::LoadAllResources(string,ResourceType)" return="array">
<member name="M:CometEngine::RuntimeAssets::LoadAllEntities(string)" return="array">
<member name="M:CometEngine::SceneManagement::SceneManager::GetSceneAsyncOperations()" return="array">
<member name="M:CometEngine::Window::GetAvailableResolutions()" return="array">
<member name="M:CometEngine::CometEventArg::RemovePersistentListeners()" return="void">
<member name="M:CometEngine::CometEventArg::Invoke(T)" return="void">
<member name="M:CometEngine::CometEventArg::AddRuntimeListener(CometDelegate+)" return="void">
<member name="M:CometEngine::CometEventArg::RemoveRuntimeListener(CometDelegate+)" return="void">
<member name="T:CometEngine::CometEventArg">
